title: Eriocaulon gardnerianum
intro: Eriocaulon gardnerianum, also known as Indian pipewort, is a plant species belonging to the family Eriocaulaceae, characterized by its small, white, and fragrant flowers and slender, grass-like foliage. Traditionally, Eriocaulon gardnerianum has been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Eriocaulon gardnerianum is commonly used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs like ginger and tulsi to enhance its therapeutic effects.
taxonomy: Eriocaulon gardnerianum is classified within the family Eriocaulaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as pipeworts or fairy cups. This family consists of approximately 750 species, mostly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Eriocaulon is further divided into several species, with Eriocaulon gardnerianum being one of the unique species within it. Eriocaulon gardnerianum is native to Southeast Asia, and its classification is supported by various studies in botany and plant taxonomy. The specific classification of Eriocaulon gardnerianum also includes its kingdom, phylum, class, order, and family as Plantae, Liliopsida, Commelinidae, Poales, and Eriocaulaceae, respectively.
